title: 0. Feminist Judgments: From the Vietnamese Perspective
originalSummary: This chapter does not focus on a specific case but introduces the concept and development of feminist legal theory in Vietnam.
feministSummary: It proposes a Vietnamese-oriented approach to feminist judging, emphasizing the role of gender, social context, and lived experiences in legal interpretation.
author: Trinh Thuc Hien

title: 1. Cases 1 and 2: Sexual Offences
court: People's Court at Ha Long City; People's Court at Bac Giang Province
originalSummary: Case No. 66/2020/HS-ST dated 24 February 2020 by the People's Court at Ha Long City on "Sexual abuse"; and Case No. 72/2020/HS-PT dated 12 June 2020 by the People's Court at Bac Giang Province on "Rape".
feministSummary: The analysis critiques limitations in access to justice and highlights the need to center victims’ experiences and gender-sensitive reasoning in adjudication.
author: Phan Thi Lan Huong

title: 2. Case 3: Dividing Common Property in Divorce
court: Judicial Council of the Supreme People's Court
originalSummary: Decision No. 02/2020/HNGD-GDT dated 8 July 2020 of the Judicial Council of the Supreme People's Court on disputes over divorce, child rearing, and common property division.
feministSummary: It re-evaluates how non-monetary contributions, particularly unpaid domestic labor by women, should be recognized in ensuring substantive equality.
author: Nguyen Thi Hong Nhung, Ngo Minh Phuong Thao

title: 3. Case 4: The Rights of the Spouses in Managing Companies After Divorce
court: People's High Court in Ho Chi Minh City
originalSummary: Case No. 39/2019/HNGD-PT dated 19 March 2018 by the People's High Court in Ho Chi Minh City about "Marriage dispute".
feministSummary: The commentary highlights gender bias in recognizing women’s economic roles and proposes a more equitable approach to ownership and management rights.
author: Doan Thi Phuong Diep, Dang Hoang Minh

title: 4. Case 5: One-Sided Divorce Filing
court: People's Court in Lam Dong Province
originalSummary: Case No. 04/2018/HNGD-PT dated 19 March 2018 by the People's Court in Lam Dong Province on "Divorce filing".
feministSummary: It examines power imbalances within marriage and argues for a more context-sensitive interpretation that better protects vulnerable parties.
author: Nguyen Dinh Duc, Trinh Thuc Hien

title: 5. Case 6: Inheritance Dispute
court: People's Court in Binh Dinh Province
originalSummary: Case No. 56/2019/DSPT dated 17 September 2019 by the People's Court in Binh Dinh Province on "Inheritance dispute and request of cancelling certificate of land use right".
feministSummary: It critiques the influence of cultural norms on women’s property rights and advocates for a more gender-equal interpretation of inheritance law.
author: Nguyen Thi Kim Chung

title: 6. Case 7: The Right of a Female Employee to Be Consulted
court: People's Court in Ho Chi Minh City
originalSummary: Case No. 722/2020/LD-PT dated 29 July 2020 by the People's Court in Ho Chi Minh City about "Dispute on applying the discipline of dismissal on employee".
feministSummary: It emphasizes the importance of women’s voices in the workplace and calls for stronger protection of female employees in labour law.
author: Dang Hoang Minh, Doan Thi Phuong Diep
